project_type: Minecraft LAN Tunnel - Electron + React desktop app with Node.js WebSocket relay server (monorepo)
build_system: npm workspaces, esbuild for relay bundling, vite + electron-builder for desktop
test_framework: vitest (unit tests at root), manual integration test script (scripts/test-integration.ts)
verification_instructions: 1. npm install, 2. npm run build (compiles all workspaces in order: shared -> tunnel-core -> relay-server -> desktop), 3. npm test (vitest), 4. Verify no TypeScript errors
snapshot_or_generated_files: apps/desktop/resources/relay-server.cjs is generated via 'npm run bundle:relay' (esbuild bundles relay-server into single CJS file for Electron). dist/ and dist-main/ are build outputs but not checked in.
environment_constraints: OPEN_INTERNET - can install packages, access registries. No electron-builder packaging test possible (requires Windows for .exe). Cannot test actual Electron launch in sandbox.
contribution_requirements: Follow existing code patterns. Ensure npm run build and npm test pass. TypeScript strict mode. UI in Russian language.
key_patterns: Settings stored in localStorage via useSettings hook in useIpc.ts. IPC handlers in src/main/index.ts communicate between Electron main and renderer. RelayServerManager forks bundled relay-server.cjs as child process. Relay URL passed as parameter to createRoom/joinRoom IPC calls. Shared constants in packages/shared/src/index.ts.
directory_structure: Monorepo: packages/shared (types, constants), packages/tunnel-core (binary framing, reconnection), apps/relay-server (WebSocket relay Node.js server), apps/desktop (Electron + React app with main/renderer processes)
